finding: Browser render-state evidence cannot establish acoustic output. A permission-gated microphone capture can provide stronger, independently falsifiable evidence when it contains the diagnostic pulse frequency and passes signal, spectral-distinctness, and clipping gates.

primary_sources.title: Media Capture and Streams
primary_sources.relevance: Defines permission-gated acquisition of microphone MediaStreams through getUserMedia.

primary_sources.title: Web Audio API
primary_sources.relevance: Defines browser audio processing and analysis primitives but does not make browser processing equivalent to physical audibility.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files
cases: clipped target capture classified as ambiguous

uncertainty: No physical iPhone microphone capture was performed.
Thresholds are initial engineering defaults and require device calibration.
Microphone detection cannot identify which transducer emitted the tone.
Acoustic detection does not prove conscious perception by a listener.
falsification_route: Run the same capture with the speaker muted or output route changed. Target-band power and target-to-neighbor ratio should materially decline below acceptance thresholds.
privacy_boundary: The detector consumes transient Float32Array samples. The implementation does not persist or publish microphone recordings, private speech, health data, credentials, or identifying content.
